temporal_range: 2020 release to 2025 release (most recent uses BRFSS 2022 + ACS 2018-2022)
iman_use_case: Tier-1 outcome tracking: diabetes, obesity, hypertension, mental distress, smoking, uninsured, routine checkup, BP/cholesterol medication adherence at Englewood + Chicago Lawn tract level. Primary dashboard input.
Match IMAN clinic catchment ZIPs (60636 60609 60621 60629 60632) to health measures when tract-level is suppressed.
Cook County benchmarking + City of Chicago totals for investor narrative.

dataset_name: USALEEP (Life Expectancy at Birth by Census Tract)
update_frequency: one-off (no refresh planned as of verification date)
iman_use_case: Canonical LE gap measure (Englewood tract 6808 vs Streeterville tract 812). Use for positive-deviant regression + peer-neighborhood matching. CRITICAL: 2010-2015 vintage pre-dates most IMAN programs — triangulate with newer proxy mortality from CDC WONDER.

geo_resolution: county (tract not available; some ZIP via dashboards)
iman_use_case: Cook County cause-specific mortality (diabetes DM I-code J44 COPD I21 MI I50 CHF X60-X84 suicide X85-Y09 assault Y90-Y98 overdose). GOTCHA: sub-county rates are suppressed when deaths < 10 in a cell (NCHS confidentiality rule) — Englewood alone will hit suppression for many causes.

direct_download_url: https://wonder.cdc.gov/ + restricted micro-data via NCHS RDC
geo_resolution: national / state / county (restricted-use micro-data has tract)
iman_use_case: Source-of-record for every published mortality rate. Use restricted micro-data only if tract-level mortality becomes essential and suppression in WONDER blocks analysis.
Chicago-specific infant mortality + birth outcomes at a cadence the city publishes faster than WONDER.

geo_resolution: Chicago community area (77) + ZIP + census tract + ward
iman_use_case: Englewood (CA 68) + West Englewood (CA 67) + Chicago Lawn / Gage Park (CA 66 + 63) side-by-side comparisons across 160+ indicators. Fastest path to a community-area vs city-mean comparison chart.

geo_resolution: MMSA (Metro / Micro Statistical Area) — Chicago-Naperville-Elgin is one MMSA
iman_use_case: Metro-level behavioral prevalence (smoking, alcohol, physical activity, fruit/veg consumption, mental distress) — for MSA context around Englewood's sub-metro estimates.
Uninsured rate time series for Cook County — policy context for IMAN clinic utilization trends.

temporal_range: 2014-2025 (note: 2026 skipped full annual release per publisher notice)
iman_use_case: County-level benchmarking for Cook County against peer-city counties (Wayne MI Philadelphia PA Baltimore MD) for investor narrative.

geo_resolution: census block / block group / tract / place / county
iman_use_case: Workhorse denominator for everything. Poverty rate, median income, race/ethnicity, educational attainment, vacant housing, vehicle access, etc. at tract level for Englewood + Chicago Lawn + peer-neighborhood pool.
Authoritative population denominators + housing unit counts (note: differential privacy noise applied at small geographies).
AGI, EITC claims, returns filed at ZIP — proxy for EITC impact story (FU3 in decision tree).

geo_resolution: state / county / MCD / place / census tract
iman_use_case: Housing cost burden by AMI — input to PSH / housing mobility / HO1-HO4 evaluations. Tract-level is available starting in 2014-2018 vintage.
Count and characteristics of voucher / public housing / Section 8 units in Englewood + Chicago Lawn tracts — for HO2 housing mobility analysis.
Affordability benchmark for Regenerator Housing + housing mobility programs.

geo_resolution: loan record geocoded to census tract
iman_use_case: Mortgage origination + denial rates in Englewood + Chicago Lawn tracts — historical disinvestment evidence + HO3/HO4 CLT + mixed-income narrative.
Intergenerational mobility by parent race + income for every US tract — matches IMAN's 2040 ED1-ED5 early-childhood ROI narrative.

geo_resolution: ZIP code + county + high school + college
iman_use_case: Economic connectedness score — proxy for IMAN's Community-building pillar (RC1-RC4).
Parcel-level assessed value, vacant-land flag, sales history — input to vacant-lot intervention (HO/RC1) effect sizes + NMTC project planning.

geo_resolution: varies (address / beat / tract / ward / community area)
iman_use_case: Parent portal for Chicago datasets below.

geo_resolution: address (masked to block) + beat + ward + community area + district
iman_use_case: Shootings, homicides, aggravated assault counts at community-area level — evaluate CP1 GVI + CP2 Cure Violence + IM3 Green Reentry.
Graffiti removal + garbage carts + rodent complaints — proxies for neighborhood disinvestment vs positive-deviant peers.
Investment activity in Englewood + Chicago Lawn — IM2 Regenerator Housing tracking + NMTC deal pipeline.
Housing quality + neglect indicator — Tier 2 for HO5 lead remediation priority mapping.
Eviction filing + judgment rates in target tracts — housing-instability mortality pathway.
Track MT2 Racine Green Line reopening impact; baseline transit access trend for peer neighborhood comparison.
LILATracts flag for Englewood + Chicago Lawn — FA3 Fresh Market Expansion justification + FA1/FA2 need estimation.
County-level food-access + food-assistance context: Cook vs peer-city counties.

temporal_range: 20-year rolling window + current active; updated Dec 31 2025
iman_use_case: Count and geocode SNAP-authorized retailers in Englewood + Chicago Lawn — input to FA1 Fresh Boost targeting.
Illinois SNAP caseload trend for FU3 policy context.
Illinois WIC caseload — FA2 WIC Access evaluation.

producer: EPA — REMOVED from EPA site Feb 2025; preserved by PEDP
landing_page_url: (EPA removed Feb 2025) archived at Harvard Dataverse + PEDP mirror
iman_use_case: PM2.5 + ozone + traffic + proximity-to-TSDF + superfund-proximity at block-group level — MT1 PM2.5 narrative + environmental justice framing. FLAG: EPA removed this tool Feb 2025; use PEDP reconstruction and Harvard archive.

geo_resolution: monitor point + lat/lon (interpolated to tract via other tools)
iman_use_case: PM2.5 + ozone + AQI for Chicago's monitoring stations — air-quality context for MT1.

temporal_range: varies by table (1981-2024 for climate; 1990-2022 for SES)
iman_use_case: Pre-built neighborhood measures (healthcare access, partisanship, SES, libraries, transit stops, PRISM climate, land-cover) — labor-saving source for peer-neighborhood features.
National peer-city crime benchmarks for Stream D project comparisons.
Incident-level shooting data including mass shootings for Chicago — supplements CPD crime data with geocoded GVA-classified events.

dataset_name: Head Start Center Locator / Service Locations
iman_use_case: ED1 Early Childhood Education — enumerate Head Start seats in Englewood + Chicago Lawn vs need.

dataset_name: SAMHSA N-SUMHSS Treatment Facility Directory (successor to N-SSATS + N-MHSS)
iman_use_case: MOUD + naloxone + behavioral-health facility supply (HC5 + HC6) — who is the competition / who is the partner in Chicago Southside.

producer: HRSA Bureau of Primary Health Care
iman_use_case: FQHC benchmark: patient count, HTN control, DM control, depression screening, prenatal visits — IMAN Health Clinic performance vs peer FQHCs.
Provider-supply context: primary care / dentist / psychiatrist counts for Cook County vs peer counties.

producer: University of Richmond DSL + Virginia Tech
iman_use_case: Historical HOLC grades for Chicago — structural cause narrative + peer-neighborhood matching variable. Englewood + Chicago Lawn both contain D-graded ("redlined") tracts.
Crosswalk ACS + Census vintages to a stable tract geography for time-series analysis.

dataset_name: Chicago Health Atlas (HCS — Healthy Chicago Survey)
iman_use_case: City-administered survey — behavioral + self-rated health at community-area resolution (more granular than BRFSS MMSA).

license: Proprietary — free tier with rate limits + paid
iman_use_case: Per-address walkability score — MT3 Safe Walking Routes + RC3 Park access context. Be cautious: proprietary methodology.
Eviction filing rate pre/post COVID — housing-instability pathway to mortality.

verified_url_date: IPR is a secondary / tertiary knowledge source — individual research products to browse for Chicago-specific working papers.

iman_use_case: Chicago regional planning + transit reform + zoning background reports — contextual not analytical.
